title: Password encryption based on a certificate
author: FrancescoBonizzi
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->

In our company we have all logins stored in a SQL storage. We coded a little application to generate an mRemotNG XML configuration based on our data. The idea is to generate that configuration once and distribute such file over our company. 

We don't understand how to encrypt correctly each remote desktop login password to make you application read it correctly. Do you have any suggestions? Maybe is there the possibility to encrypt / decrypt such passwords with a c...

iterations.description: Feature request for certificate-based password encryption lacks concrete specification (which certificate store, X.509 vs DPAPI, key management workflow) and has no recent community demand beyond the legacy triage refresh comment

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Feature request for certificate-based password encryption lacks concrete specification (which certificate store, X.509 vs DPAPI, key management workflow) and has no recent community demand beyond the legacy triage refresh comment
Approach: Would require a new ICryptographyProvider implementation using X.509 certificate public/private key pairs (RSA/ECDH) to wrap the symmetric encryption key, plus UI for certificate selection and key escrow strategy — needs detailed design spec and user input before implementation
